title: pull / push to a registry behind an apache proxy
body:
We have an apache behind which different services run :
mydomain / service1
mydomain / service2
...
but also a registry docker. 
For example to pull an image docker, docker pull mydomain / myimage the redirection will be IP: 9037 / myimage
But to distinguish the processing that apache must do to distinguish a mydomain / service1 call from mydomain / myimage we use the user agent label of http / https calls  :

'''
 RewriteCond% {HTTP_USER_AGENT} "(Docker-Client | docker)"
    RewriteRule ^ / (. *) Http://10.27.221.88:9040/$1 [P]
'''
When docker tries to join the register in https or http, a distinctive label "docker or Docker-Client" is passed as user agent header. Kubernetes-type platforms also use the HTTP_USER_AGENT header to communicate and distinguish the origin of the call.
On the other hand, buildah passes as user agent "go-http-client" which is not enough discriminating so that the apache does well redirection.

Is it possible to consider that buildah calls to registers will be done with a distinctive HTTP_USER_AGENT header  to join a registry behind an Apache frontend.

Sent from Gmail Mobile on iPhone
@ipbabble did you take a look at this?

to easily reproduce:

```console
$ nc --ssl -l 8080
GET /v2/ HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost:8080
User-Agent: Go-http-client/1.1
Docker-Distribution-Api-Version: registry/2.0
Accept-Encoding: gzip
Connection: close
```
and from another terminal:

```console
buildah push --tls-verify=false  fedora localhost:8080/foo
```

PR here: https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/814
